texts.orig: G. S. Golubev a, *, I. L. Borisov a , and V. V. Volkov a a  Topchiev Institute of Petrochemical Synthesis, Russian Academy of Sciences, Moscow, 119991 Russia *e-mail: GolubevGS@ips.ac.ru
texts.text: G. S. Golubev a, *, I. L. Borisov a , and V. V. Volkov a a  Topchiev Institute of Petrochemical Synthesis, Russian Academy of Sciences, Moscow, 119991 Russia *e-mail: GolubevGS@ips.ac.ru

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Received May 15, 2018
texts.text: Received May 15, 2018

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Abstract -A poly[1-(trimethylsilyl)-1-propyne] membrane was studied in a thermopervaporation process for ethanol recovery from fermentation media. Four commercial composite membranes based on polysiloxanes (Pervap 4060, Pervatech PDMS, PolyAn, and MDK-3) were studied for comparison. The dependences of the permeate fl  ux, permeate concentration, separation factor, and pervaporation separation index on the temperature of the feed mixture (5 wt % ethanol in water) were obtained. The maximal values of the ethanol concentration in the permeate (35 wt %) and separation factor (10.2) were obtained for the poly[1-(trimethylsilyl)-1-propyne] membrane, whereas the PolyAn membrane provided the highest permeate fl  ux (5.4 kg m -2  h -1 ). The ethanol/ water separation factor for the systems studied has a maximum at 60° С ; this temperature of the feed mixture is optimum for recovering ethanol from aqueous media by thermopervaporation. The existing membranes based on polysiloxanes show low ethanol-water selectivity (less than 1). Poly[1-(trimethylsilyl)-1-propyne] membranes are the most promising for recovering bioethanol from fermentation mixtures by thermopervaporation, because they showed the highest selectivity to ethanol.
texts.text: Abstract -A poly[1-(trimethylsilyl)-1-propyne] membrane was studied in a thermopervaporation process for ethanol recovery from fermentation media. Four commercial composite membranes based on polysiloxanes (Pervap 4060, Pervatech PDMS, PolyAn, and MDK-3) were studied for comparison. The dependences of the permeate fl  ux, permeate concentration, separation factor, and pervaporation separation index on the temperature of the feed mixture (5 wt % ethanol in water) were obtained. The maximal values of the ethanol concentration in the permeate (35 wt %) and separation factor (10.2) were obtained for the poly[1-(trimethylsilyl)-1-propyne] membrane, whereas the PolyAn membrane provided the highest permeate fl  ux (5.4 kg m -2  h -1 ). The ethanol/ water separation factor for the systems studied has a maximum at 60° С ; this temperature of the feed mixture is optimum for recovering ethanol from aqueous media by thermopervaporation. The existing membranes based on polysiloxanes show low ethanol-water selectivity (less than 1). Poly[1-(trimethylsilyl)-1-propyne] membranes are the most promising for recovering bioethanol from fermentation mixtures by thermopervaporation, because they showed the highest selectivity to ethanol.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Keywords: thermopervaporation, ethanol, fermentation mixtures, polysiloxanes, poly[1-(trimethylsilyl)-1-pro- pyne]
texts.text: Keywords: thermopervaporation, ethanol, fermentation mixtures, polysiloxanes, poly[1-(trimethylsilyl)-1-pro- pyne]

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The interest in renewable power sources grows year by year because of exhaustion of traditional resources and  negative  impact  of  the  human  activity  on  the environment  [1,  2].  Ethanol  is  the  most  widely  used liquid biofuel; it is produced by biomass fermentation [3, 4]. In addition, ethanol is widely used in medicine and in chemical, perfumery, and food industry [5].
texts.text: The interest in renewable power sources grows year by year because of exhaustion of traditional resources and  negative  impact  of  the  human  activity  on  the environment  [1,  2].  Ethanol  is  the  most  widely  used liquid biofuel; it is produced by biomass fermentation [3, 4]. In addition, ethanol is widely used in medicine and in chemical, perfumery, and food industry [5].

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Bioethanol is produced by fermentation of organic products  containing  carbohydrates  under  the  action of  yeast  enzymes.  However,  because  of  the  inhibiting effect  of  the  fermentation  products  on  yeast,  the  fi  nal ethanol concentration in the fermentation solution does not exceed 15 wt % [6, 7].
texts.text: Bioethanol is produced by fermentation of organic products  containing  carbohydrates  under  the  action of  yeast  enzymes.  However,  because  of  the  inhibiting effect  of  the  fermentation  products  on  yeast,  the  fi  nal ethanol concentration in the fermentation solution does not exceed 15 wt % [6, 7].

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Therefore, the problem with recovering fermentation products is one of the major factors preventing wider use of bioethanol in industry and on transport. Distillation traditionally used for these purposes is expensive because of  large  power  consumption  [8].  Several  alternative methods were suggested for recovering alcohols from fermentation mixture: adsorption [9, 10], removal with gas  stripping[11,  12],  extraction  [13-15],  membrane distillation  [16,  17],  and  pervaporation  [2,  8,  18,  19]. Pervaporation is the most promising approach to ethanol recovery from fermentation mixtures, because it exerts no negative effect on microorganisms and ensures high separation characteristics [18].
texts.text: Therefore, the problem with recovering fermentation products is one of the major factors preventing wider use of bioethanol in industry and on transport. Distillation traditionally used for these purposes is expensive because of  large  power  consumption  [8].  Several  alternative methods were suggested for recovering alcohols from fermentation mixture: adsorption [9, 10], removal with gas  stripping[11,  12],  extraction  [13-15],  membrane distillation  [16,  17],  and  pervaporation  [2,  8,  18,  19]. Pervaporation is the most promising approach to ethanol recovery from fermentation mixtures, because it exerts no negative effect on microorganisms and ensures high separation characteristics [18].

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Thermopervaporation (TPV), one of the ways of  utilizing  the  driving  force  of  pervaporation,  was suggested previously for recovering alcohols  from fermentation mixtures [20-26]. As compared to vacuum pervaporation, in TPV there is no problem with infl  ow of permanent gases in the course of recovering alcohols
texts.text: Thermopervaporation (TPV), one of the ways of  utilizing  the  driving  force  of  pervaporation,  was suggested previously for recovering alcohols  from fermentation mixtures [20-26]. As compared to vacuum pervaporation, in TPV there is no problem with infl  ow of permanent gases in the course of recovering alcohols

texts.orig: from fermentation media, and the permeate condensation occurs directly in the membrane unit on the cold surface near the membrane at atmospheric pressure [21].

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Active efforts are made in the worldwide to develop new membrane materials providing high values of both permeate fl  ux and separation factors for enhancing the effi  ciency of recovering bioalcohols from fermentation mixtures. For example, hydrophobic membrane materials based on poly(dimethylsiloxane) (PDMS) and poly[1-(trimethylsilyl)-1-propyne] (PTMSP) showed high separation characteristics in recovering bioalcohols from aqueous media [27-32]. Pervap 4060 membrane showed the highest performance in pervaporation recovery  of  ethanol  from  aqueous  media,  exhibiting 3  times  higher  separation  factor  (11.6)  compared  to Pervatech  PDMS  (3.6)  and  PolyAn  (3.4)  membranes [27]. This fact is also confi  rmed by Chovau et al. [28], who obtained for the commercial Pervap 4060 higher separation factor in ethanol recovery from fermentation mixtures (8.4) compared to Pervatech PDMS membrane (5.2).
texts.text: Active efforts are made in the worldwide to develop new membrane materials providing high values of both permeate fl  ux and separation factors for enhancing the effi  ciency of recovering bioalcohols from fermentation mixtures. For example, hydrophobic membrane materials based on poly(dimethylsiloxane) (PDMS) and poly[1-(trimethylsilyl)-1-propyne] (PTMSP) showed high separation characteristics in recovering bioalcohols from aqueous media [27-32]. Pervap 4060 membrane showed the highest performance in pervaporation recovery  of  ethanol  from  aqueous  media,  exhibiting 3  times  higher  separation  factor  (11.6)  compared  to Pervatech  PDMS  (3.6)  and  PolyAn  (3.4)  membranes [27]. This fact is also confi  rmed by Chovau et al. [28], who obtained for the commercial Pervap 4060 higher separation factor in ethanol recovery from fermentation mixtures (8.4) compared to Pervatech PDMS membrane (5.2).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A separation factor of 10.7 was obtained in pervaporation  separation  of  a  10  wt  %  solution  of ethanol in water with a 100μ m-thick PTMSP membrane [29].  However, in the course of the 450-h experiment the  separation  factor  gradually  decreased  to  8.  This decrease  in  the  transport  properties  was  attributed  to relaxation processes in the PTMSP membrane, altering the membrane structure and morphology.
texts.text: A separation factor of 10.7 was obtained in pervaporation  separation  of  a  10  wt  %  solution  of ethanol in water with a 100μ m-thick PTMSP membrane [29].  However, in the course of the 450-h experiment the  separation  factor  gradually  decreased  to  8.  This decrease  in  the  transport  properties  was  attributed  to relaxation processes in the PTMSP membrane, altering the membrane structure and morphology.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: These  membrane  materials  have  yet  been  poorly studied  in  TPV  recovery  of  alcohols  from  aqueous media. Only a few studies deal with the ethanol recovery from aqueous media by thermopervaporation using two commercial PDMS membranes: Pervatech PDMS and Pervap 4060 [33, 34]. There are still no studies on TPV recovery of ethanol using PTMSP membranes.
texts.text: These  membrane  materials  have  yet  been  poorly studied  in  TPV  recovery  of  alcohols  from  aqueous media. Only a few studies deal with the ethanol recovery from aqueous media by thermopervaporation using two commercial PDMS membranes: Pervatech PDMS and Pervap 4060 [33, 34]. There are still no studies on TPV recovery of ethanol using PTMSP membranes.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This study was aimed at comparing the performance of four commercially available composite membranes based on  polysiloxanes  and  PTMSP  in  ethanol  recovery  from model fermentation mixtures by thermopervaporation.
texts.text: This study was aimed at comparing the performance of four commercially available composite membranes based on  polysiloxanes  and  PTMSP  in  ethanol  recovery  from model fermentation mixtures by thermopervaporation.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: EXPERIMENTAL
texts.text: EXPERIMENTAL

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Membranes based on PTMSP and composite membranes  based  on  PDMS  were  used  in  the  study. The  following  commercial  samples  were  chosen  as composite  PDMS  membranes:  Pervap  4060  (Sulzer Chemtech, Switzerland), Pervatech PDMS (Pervatech, Netherlands),  PolyAn  POL_OR_M2  (PolyAn  GmbH, Germany), and MDK-3 (Vladipor, Russia).
texts.text: Membranes based on PTMSP and composite membranes  based  on  PDMS  were  used  in  the  study. The  following  commercial  samples  were  chosen  as composite  PDMS  membranes:  Pervap  4060  (Sulzer Chemtech, Switzerland), Pervatech PDMS (Pervatech, Netherlands),  PolyAn  POL_OR_M2  (PolyAn  GmbH, Germany), and MDK-3 (Vladipor, Russia).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: PTMSP samples  were  purchased  from  Gelest  (the United States). The membranes were prepared by casting a 1.5 wt % solution of the polymer in chloroform onto Cellophane, followed by drying at room temperature for 200 h. The diameter of the initial fi  lms was 7.5 cm, and the thickness was varied in the interval 30-35 μ m. The membrane thickness was measured with a Mitutoyo® 293 Digimatic QuickMike electronic micrometer with ±1 μ m accuracy.
texts.text: PTMSP samples  were  purchased  from  Gelest  (the United States). The membranes were prepared by casting a 1.5 wt % solution of the polymer in chloroform onto Cellophane, followed by drying at room temperature for 200 h. The diameter of the initial fi  lms was 7.5 cm, and the thickness was varied in the interval 30-35 μ m. The membrane thickness was measured with a Mitutoyo® 293 Digimatic QuickMike electronic micrometer with ±1 μ m accuracy.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: thermopervaporation unit 3 .  The  fi  rst  circuit  in  which a  coolant  circulates  is  closed  through  a  cryostat.  The second  circuit  consists  of  a  vessel  with  a  feed  (2-L fl  ask), placed in a thermostat heating the mixture to the preset temperature, and of a peristaltic pump ensuring the liquid circulation in the circuit. The temperature is maintained with ±0.2°C accuracy. The temperatures of the feed and coolant at the inlet and outlet of the TPV unit were measured with thermometers.
texts.text: thermopervaporation unit 3 .  The  fi  rst  circuit  in  which a  coolant  circulates  is  closed  through  a  cryostat.  The second  circuit  consists  of  a  vessel  with  a  feed  (2-L fl  ask), placed in a thermostat heating the mixture to the preset temperature, and of a peristaltic pump ensuring the liquid circulation in the circuit. The temperature is maintained with ±0.2°C accuracy. The temperatures of the feed and coolant at the inlet and outlet of the TPV unit were measured with thermometers.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The  unit  consists  of  two  mirror-symmetrical  parts separated by membrane 5 and condensation surface 6 , between which there is an air gap. The permeate was condensed  on  the  metal  plate  and  removed  from  the unit by gravity into permeate collector 4 . The effective surface area of the membrane was 25.5 cm 2 .
texts.text: The  unit  consists  of  two  mirror-symmetrical  parts separated by membrane 5 and condensation surface 6 , between which there is an air gap. The permeate was condensed  on  the  metal  plate  and  removed  from  the unit by gravity into permeate collector 4 . The effective surface area of the membrane was 25.5 cm 2 .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Thermopervaporation  was  performed  at  the  feed temperature T F =  40-80  (±0.2)° С ;  the  pressure  in the  undermembrane  space  was  equal  to  atmospheric pressure. The permeate was condensed at T c = 10° С . The air gap width was 2.5 mm. A binary 5.0 wt % solution of ethanol in water was used in separation experiments.
texts.text: Thermopervaporation  was  performed  at  the  feed temperature T F =  40-80  (±0.2)° С ;  the  pressure  in the  undermembrane  space  was  equal  to  atmospheric pressure. The permeate was condensed at T c = 10° С . The air gap width was 2.5 mm. A binary 5.0 wt % solution of ethanol in water was used in separation experiments.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: All  solutions  were  prepared  gravimetrically  from organic solvents (chemically pure grade).
texts.text: All  solutions  were  prepared  gravimetrically  from organic solvents (chemically pure grade).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The  concentrations  of  the  initial  mixture  and  permeate were determined by gas chromatography with a Crystallux-4000 М chromatograph equipped with a thermal conductivity detector. Chromatographic separation was performed under the following conditions: vaporizer  temperature  230°C,  column  temperature  180°C, detector temperature 230°C, and length of the column packed with Porapak Q 1 m.
texts.text: The  concentrations  of  the  initial  mixture  and  permeate were determined by gas chromatography with a Crystallux-4000 М chromatograph equipped with a thermal conductivity detector. Chromatographic separation was performed under the following conditions: vaporizer  temperature  230°C,  column  temperature  180°C, detector temperature 230°C, and length of the column packed with Porapak Q 1 m.

texts.orig: The thermopervaporation  effi  ciency  was  characterized  by  the  pervaporation  separation  index  PSI  [23, 27, 35]: PSI = J ( α -  1),  which takes into account two factors: permeate fl  ux and separation factor.
texts.text: The thermopervaporation  effi  ciency  was  characterized  by  the  pervaporation  separation  index  PSI  [23, 27, 35]: PSI = J ( α -  1),  which takes into account two factors: permeate fl  ux and separation factor.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: To  determine  the  vapor  pressures  of  the  permeate and  initial  ethanol-water  mixture,  we  calculated  the activity  coeffi  cients  using  the  Non-Random  Two  Liquid (NRTL) model and Aspen Plus V8.6 software. This calculation method is widely applied to water-alcohol systems [27, 37].

This calculation method is widely applied to water-alcohol systems [27, 37].

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: RESULTS  AND  DISCUSSION
texts.text: RESULTS  AND  DISCUSSION

texts.label: text
texts.orig: An important factor infl  uencing the pervaporation characteristics of the separation process is the temperature of the feed. Therefore, we performed TPV experiments  with  four  composite  membranes  based on PDMS and of the continuous 33μ m-thick PTMSP membrane at varied temperature of the aqueous ethanol solution being separated (Fig. 2).
texts.text: An important factor infl  uencing the pervaporation characteristics of the separation process is the temperature of the feed. Therefore, we performed TPV experiments  with  four  composite  membranes  based on PDMS and of the continuous 33μ m-thick PTMSP membrane at varied temperature of the aqueous ethanol solution being separated (Fig. 2).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Figure 2a shows how the permeate composition in thermopervaporation separation of a 5 wt % solution of ethanol in water with all the tested membranes varies with the solution temperature. Irrespective of the solution  temperature,  the  maximal  concentrations  of ethanol in the permeate were obtained for the PTMSP membrane. Among commercial membranes, the highest concentration of ethanol in the permeate (26.8 wt %), as in vacuum pervaporation [27, 28], was reached with

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The  permeate  fl  ux  increases  nonlinearly  with  an increase in the feed temperature (Fig. 2b); irrespective of temperature, the highest permeate fl  uxes were obtained with  PolyAn  membrane.  For  example,  at  80° С the permeate fl  ux was 5.4 kg m -2  h -1 , being virtually 2 times higher  compared  to  MDK-3,  Pervatech  PDMS,  and Pervap 4060 membranes and more than 4 times higher compared  to  PTMSP  membrane.  This  fact  suggests that the selective layer of the PolyAn membrane is the thinnest and/or the least cross-linked, compared to the other commercial membranes being tested; this was also noted in [27].
texts.text: The  permeate  fl  ux  increases  nonlinearly  with  an increase in the feed temperature (Fig. 2b); irrespective of temperature, the highest permeate fl  uxes were obtained with  PolyAn  membrane.  For  example,  at  80° С the permeate fl  ux was 5.4 kg m -2  h -1 , being virtually 2 times higher  compared  to  MDK-3,  Pervatech  PDMS,  and Pervap 4060 membranes and more than 4 times higher compared  to  PTMSP  membrane.  This  fact  suggests that the selective layer of the PolyAn membrane is the thinnest and/or the least cross-linked, compared to the other commercial membranes being tested; this was also noted in [27].

texts.orig: Figure  2c  shows  the  separation  factors  calculated using Eq. (2) for the membranes being tested in TPV separation  of  an  ethanol-water  binary  mixture.  The highest  separation  factor  (10.2)  was  obtained  for  the PTMSP  membrane  at  a  separation  temperature  of
texts.text: Figure  2c  shows  the  separation  factors  calculated using Eq. (2) for the membranes being tested in TPV separation  of  an  ethanol-water  binary  mixture.  The highest  separation  factor  (10.2)  was  obtained  for  the PTMSP  membrane  at  a  separation  temperature  of

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 60° С . In addition, Figs. 2a and 2c show that, for all the membranes, the ethanol concentration in the permeate and  the  ethanol-water  separation  factor  pass  through a  maximum  at  60° С .  This  fact  is  important  from  the viewpoint of thermopervaporation optimization.
texts.text: 60° С . In addition, Figs. 2a and 2c show that, for all the membranes, the ethanol concentration in the permeate and  the  ethanol-water  separation  factor  pass  through a  maximum  at  60° С .  This  fact  is  important  from  the viewpoint of thermopervaporation optimization.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The maximum of the separation factor is determined as the product of two quantities: phase transition separation factor and membrane permselectivity [23, 38]. The fi  rst  quantity  is  determined  by  the  liquid-vapor  thermodynamic equilibrium and is proportional to the ratio of the driving forces of the two components passing through the membrane, and the second quantity is determined by the transport characteristics of the membrane.
texts.text: The maximum of the separation factor is determined as the product of two quantities: phase transition separation factor and membrane permselectivity [23, 38]. The fi  rst  quantity  is  determined  by  the  liquid-vapor  thermodynamic equilibrium and is proportional to the ratio of the driving forces of the two components passing through the membrane, and the second quantity is determined by the transport characteristics of the membrane.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The phase transition separation factor increases with the  feed  temperature,  whereas  the  permselectivity  of the  membranes  tested  decreases  (Fig.  3). As  a  result, the separation factor as the product of these quantities has  a  maximum  at  60° С .  Thus,  this  temperature  is optimum for recovering ethanol from aqueous media by thermopervaporation.
texts.text: The phase transition separation factor increases with the  feed  temperature,  whereas  the  permselectivity  of the  membranes  tested  decreases  (Fig.  3). As  a  result, the separation factor as the product of these quantities has  a  maximum  at  60° С .  Thus,  this  temperature  is optimum for recovering ethanol from aqueous media by thermopervaporation.

The PolyAn membranes with the PDMS selective layer showed the highest PSI values in recovery of ethanol from aqueous

texts.label: text
texts.orig: To compare the results obtained with the published data,  we  calculated  the  permeabilities  for  ethanol  and water  and  the  ethanol-water  selectivity  (see  table).  It should  be  noted  that,  in  contrast  to  the  permeate  fl  ux and separation factor, the component  permeability and selectivity only weakly depend on the experiment conditions  [36].  Among  the  commercial  membranes tested  in  this  study,  PolyAn  membrane  shows  the  maximal permeability not only for ethanol (2.6 mol m -2  h -1  kPa 1 ), but also for water (5.4 mol m -2  h -1  kPa -1 ); therefore, the ethanol/water selectivity is low (0.48). The Pervap 4060 membrane shows the highest selectivity to ethanol (0.76) among commercial membranes. It can be seen, however, that all the membranes based on PDMS have low  alcohol-water  selectivity  (less  than  1);  therefore, their  use  for  pervaporation  recovery  of  ethanol  from aqueous media is not appropriate. The same is noted for another siloxane polymer, polyoctylmethylsiloxane, for which the alcohol-water selectivity is 0.47.
texts.text: To compare the results obtained with the published data,  we  calculated  the  permeabilities  for  ethanol  and water  and  the  ethanol-water  selectivity  (see  table).  It should  be  noted  that,  in  contrast  to  the  permeate  fl  ux and separation factor, the component  permeability and selectivity only weakly depend on the experiment conditions  [36].  Among  the  commercial  membranes tested  in  this  study,  PolyAn  membrane  shows  the  maximal permeability not only for ethanol (2.6 mol m -2  h -1  kPa 1 ), but also for water (5.4 mol m -2  h -1  kPa -1 ); therefore, the ethanol/water selectivity is low (0.48). The Pervap 4060 membrane shows the highest selectivity to ethanol (0.76) among commercial membranes. It can be seen, however, that all the membranes based on PDMS have low  alcohol-water  selectivity  (less  than  1);  therefore, their  use  for  pervaporation  recovery  of  ethanol  from aqueous media is not appropriate. The same is noted for another siloxane polymer, polyoctylmethylsiloxane, for which the alcohol-water selectivity is 0.47.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: PTMSP  membranes  show  high  selectivity.  Thus, PTMSP is today the most promising material for TPV recovery of bioethanol from fermentation mixtures.
texts.text: PTMSP  membranes  show  high  selectivity.  Thus, PTMSP is today the most promising material for TPV recovery of bioethanol from fermentation mixtures.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: CONCLUSIONS
texts.text: CONCLUSIONS

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Poly[1-(trimethylsilyl)-1-propyne]  membrane  was tested for the fi  rst time in thermopervaporation recovery of  ethanol  from  aqueous  media.  Four  commercial composite membranes based on polysiloxanes (Pervap 4060,  Pervatech  PDMS,  PolyAn,  and  MDK-3)  were studied for comparison.
texts.text: Poly[1-(trimethylsilyl)-1-propyne]  membrane  was tested for the fi  rst time in thermopervaporation recovery of  ethanol  from  aqueous  media.  Four  commercial composite membranes based on polysiloxanes (Pervap 4060,  Pervatech  PDMS,  PolyAn,  and  MDK-3)  were studied for comparison.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The infl  uence of the feed temperature on the thermopervaporation characteristics of the membranes was studied. The existing membranes based on polysiloxanes  exhibit  low  ethanol-water  selectivity; therefore, their use for pervaporation recovery of ethanol from aqueous media is not appropriate.
texts.text: The infl  uence of the feed temperature on the thermopervaporation characteristics of the membranes was studied. The existing membranes based on polysiloxanes  exhibit  low  ethanol-water  selectivity; therefore, their use for pervaporation recovery of ethanol from aqueous media is not appropriate.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Poly[1-(trimethylsily)-1-propyne] is today the most promising material for thermopervaporation recovery of bioethanol from fermentation mixtures.
texts.text: Poly[1-(trimethylsily)-1-propyne] is today the most promising material for thermopervaporation recovery of bioethanol from fermentation mixtures.
